steps.title: Interpret the function and statement
steps.content:
The function $f(x) = 250,000(1.20)^x$ outputs predicted annual revenue in dollars, where $x$ is years after the company started.

The notation $f(4)$ represents the revenue at year 4, a snapshot value, not a growth factor.

The statement f(4) is approximately equal to 518,400 means this revenue is approximately \$518,400.

steps.title: Identify the correct choice
steps.content:
This directly matches the correct answer, A. We can eliminate the other choices:
  • "$4$ years after the company started selling outdoor gear online, its predicted annual revenue will have increased by a total of approximately $518,400$ dollars.": Refers to cumulative increase from start, not the actual revenue at year 4.
  • "When the company's predicted annual revenue is approximately $518,400$ dollars, it is $4$ times the predicted annual revenue for the previous year.": Claims the revenue is 4 times the prior year, but base 1.20 means 1.20 times the prior year (20% annual growth), not 4.
  • "When the company's predicted annual revenue is approximately $518,400$ dollars, it is $4\%$ greater than the predicted annual revenue for the previous year.": Claims 4% annual growth, but the base 1.20 indicates 20% annual growth.
That matches the correct answer, A is correct.
